tiles.grass: Dense grass sways in the hot wind. Something moved there a moment ago.
tiles.dirt: Packed earth, marked with enormous footprints.
tiles.water: Murky water. Reflections ripple — from below, not above.
tiles.stone: Jagged rocks, warm to the touch. A good vantage point.
tiles.sand: Fine sand muffles footsteps. Tracks appear and vanish.

trex: The ground trembles. A low growl rolls across the canopy.
Something massive just displaced a hundred trees.
raptor: Chittering from the undergrowth. Too many legs moving at once.
Red eyes blink in the shadows between the ferns.
brontosaur: A shadow falls over the colony — slow, gentle, enormous.
The trees part like curtains. A mountain with a heartbeat.
pterodactyl: A shriek from above. The sky has teeth today.
Wings blot out the sun. Then gone.

settlerAttack: Steel meets scale. {attacker} does not flinch.
settlerDeath: {name} falls. The jungle swallows the silence.
dinoDeath: The {species} collapses. The ground shudders one last time.

firstBuilding: A structure rises from the wilderness. It is not much, but it is ours.
Walls against the wild. The first of many, perhaps.
firstResource: Wood. Stone. The beginnings of civilization.
survivedDino: The predator retreats. We live to see another dawn.
That was too close. But we are still here.

settlerThoughts: The wind carries the scent of damp earth... and something primal.
I should eat soon. The jungle does not wait for the weak.
Every sound could be a bird. Or a mouth with teeth.
The stars here are wrong. I do not recognize any of them.
If I die here, the trees will grow over my bones within a week.
Another sunrise. Another day not being eaten. Progress.
The Rex watches from the ridgeline. It is patient. Too patient.
I found tracks this morning. Three different species. None friendly.
The warehouse is full. If only safety could be stored alongside the wood.
At night, they call to each other across the valley. I pretend not to hear.
The ferns part behind me. I do not turn around.
Hunger is a slow enemy. Slower than any dinosaur, but just as deadly.
This clearing was someone's home once. The bones suggest it did not end well.
I talk to myself now. When you are the only human for miles, it is either that or scream.
The river runs brown after the rain. Probably just sediment. Probably.
intro: The drop pod carved a scar through the canopy.
When the smoke cleared, there was nothing but jungle in every direction.
The radio is dead. The navigation is dead. Only the mission remains:
The dinosaurs were not part of the briefing.

screens.text:
The drop pod carved a scar through the canopy.

When the smoke cleared, there was nothing but jungle in every direction.

The radio is dead. The navigation is dead.

Only the mission remains:

Build. Survive. Signal for extraction.

The dinosaurs were not part of the briefing.

LEFT CLICK — select entities, move your settler, collect resources.

BUILD — select a structure from the bottom panel, then click a tile.

INFO — click any entity to see details.

Gather wood and stone. Build a shelter.

Keep your settler alive.

The jungle remembers those who fail.
